text.title: Quality Management — Tools, Services, and References
text.summary: CLI tools, SaaS services, best practices, and references for automated quality management.

list.item: sonar-scanner — static analysis, code smells, coverage (https://docs.sonarsource.com)
ruff — Python lint + format; replaces black + flake8 (pip install ruff)
eslint — JS/TS lint (npm i -D eslint)
pytest --cov — Python coverage (pip install pytest-cov)
c8 / nyc — JS/TS coverage (npm i -D c8)
mutmut / stryker — mutation testing for critical modules (pip install mutmut / npm i -D @stryker-mutator/core)
gitleaks — secret scanning; required in the quality gate (https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks)
semgrep — pattern-based static analysis (https://semgrep.dev)
trivy — container/SBOM vulnerability scan (https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy)
gh pr checks — read CI quality gate status (https://cli.github.com)
SonarQube / SonarCloud — Web API for issues, quality gates, coverage; OSS and SaaS options.
Codacy — REST API, multi-language analysis (SaaS).
Code Climate — maintainability + test coverage, GitHub-native (SaaS).
Codecov — coverage trend API, PR comments (SaaS).
Sentry / OSS — defect-in-prod telemetry, API for events; rate-limits apply during spikes.
BrowserStack — cross-browser test runs via API; physical device testing.
TestRail — test case management, REST API; seat-priced.

rule.statement: Re-tune quality gates quarterly using lead-time and defect data — gates that are too strict kill velocity; gates that are too loose allow silent degradation.
rule.rationale: Quality cost is U-shaped: the optimal gate tightness shifts as the codebase matures and team size changes. Quarterly calibration prevents ossification.

rule.statement: For agent-generated code, raise coverage bar to 90% and require mutation testing on critical modules — LLM-generated tests often pass coverage but miss edge cases.
rule.rationale: LLMs produce plausible-looking tests that assert tautologies; mutation testing reveals whether assertions actually catch defects.

list.item: ISO 9001:2015 — quality management systems
PMBoK 7th — Delivery Performance Domain
W.E. Deming, "Out of the Crisis" (1986) — prevention over inspection
Capers Jones, "Software Quality in 2024" — defect economics
Google SRE Workbook, ch. 4 — SLOs and quality
Sibling methodologies: change-control/, code-review/, usability-testing/
